commands.review_summary:
Adds Telegram direct-message long-turn deferral state, deferred terminal delivery, reply-fence/freshness exceptions, and focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: Partly: source inspection and the PR's mocked regression tests exercise the missing long-turn/freshness path. We do not yet have a live current-main Telegram reproduction or after-fix transport run.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/live Telegram proof and maintainer acceptance of the message-delivery risk; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: fix(telegram): deliver deferred long-turn replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram direct-message turns that run past the inbound handler deadline can leave the user without a durable final/failure response, and older foreground reply freshness can cancel valid deferred finals.
- Solution: Add an explicit Telegram long-turn delivery state that sends a soft-deadline deferral, lets the run continue in the background, and delivers a terminal final/failure/silent-completion message back to the same chat/thread.
- What changed: Telegram dispatch now gates deferral notices, protects deferred reply fences from ordinary supersedes, still honors explicit aborts, and uses a private internal foreground-freshness symbol so deferred finals can survive shared reply suppression without exposing a public SDK option.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Group turns are not deferred in this PR, so existing group silence/NO_REPLY behavior stays intact; /status, /abort command UX, dedupe, and live Telegram proof remain follow-up work.

## Motivation

- This is PR B from the upstream reliability handoff for OPS-0pgd, after replay-invalid recovery in #85362. It closes the direct Telegram long-turn delivery gap before the later auth-profile, config-overlay, and observability PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85362
- Related OPS-0pgd
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram direct-message turns that exceed the soft deadline now send a run-id deferral, continue dispatch outside the inbound handler, and always resolve the visible deferral with a final, failure, or silent-completion terminal message unless explicitly aborted.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout, Node v26.0.0, pnpm 11.2.2, branch head `cc95ad551a4973e7b01b085043fc19976be8ab03` rebased onto `origin/main` `8d1ab83cb3`. The exact Telegram dispatch/fence path was exercised through the repo Vitest harness with Telegram transport calls mocked; no real Telegram credentials were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal output from the focused post-fix run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/dr/workspace/openclaw-long-turn

Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  118 passed (118)
  Start at  18:40:38
  Duration  9.41s (transform 3.63s, setup 552ms, import 2.38s, tests 6.11s, environment 0ms)

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.78)
I found no actionable defects introduced by the change bundle. The long-turn deferral state, reply-fence protection, and foreground freshness override appear scoped to preserving deferred Telegram deliveries while keeping explicit aborts effective.
```

- Observed result after fix: Regression coverage shows deferred direct turns wait for the deferral notice before final delivery, survive later ordinary same-session messages, still stop on explicit abort, release reply-fence state if fallback delivery throws, preserve group silence, and resolve deferred NO_REPLY completion with a terminal completion message instead of leaving the initial deferral hanging.
- What was not tested: A live Telegram bot with real Telegram/OpenAI credentials was not run. Pre-submit broad remote `pnpm check:changed` was attempted but blocked locally: Blacksmith Testbox needed a missing `blacksmith` CLI, and direct AWS Crabbox failed because AWS SSO credentials were expired. PR CI should provide the broad Linux gate.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Handoff OPS-0pgd documents the missing durable final delivery after the soft deadline; previous code returned from the inbound handler without a durable continuation/final-delivery contract for direct Telegram turns.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram processing was bounded by the inbound handler lifecycle and reply-freshness fences that treated all older foreground replies as stale, with no explicit long-turn state tying a visible deferral to a later terminal delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There were no Telegram tests for soft-deadline deferral ordering, deferred final freshness, abort-vs-normal supersede, fallback-send failures, or intentional silent completion after a deferral notice.
- Contributing context (if known): Shared foreground freshness is correct for ordinary turns but needed an internal channel-owned escape hatch for already-deferred runs.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: long direct turn defers after the soft deadline, final/failure/silent-completion arrives after the deferral notice, later ordinary messages do not cancel deferred finals, explicit aborts still cancel, and groups remain silent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These files exercise the Telegram processor, dispatch delivery seam, Telegram reply-fence state, and shared foreground freshness gate without needing live credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Direct Telegram users can now see `Still working on this... Run: <id>` after a long turn exceeds the soft deadline. That visible deferral is followed by the eventual assistant response, a failure message with run/session/target context, or a terminal silent-completion message when the run intentionally produced no visible reply. Group chats keep existing non-deferred silence behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[direct Telegram message] -> [soft deadline exceeded] -> [handler returns] -> [final can be lost or suppressed]

After:
[direct Telegram message] -> [soft deadline exceeded] -> [deferral notice with run id]
                          -> [background dispatch continues]
                          -> [final / failure / silent-completion terminal message]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local source checkout
- Runtime/container: Node v26.0.0, pnpm 11.2.2
- Model/provider: none live; mocked dispatch/transport in Vitest
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram plugin dispatch path
- Relevant config (redacted): no real credentials used

### Steps

1. Trigger a direct Telegram dispatch that remains pending past `TELEGRAM_LONG_TURN_SOFT_DEADLINE_MS`.
2. Confirm the processor sends the deferral notice and releases the inbound handler.
3. Resolve the background dispatcher through final, failure, explicit abort, intentional NO_REPLY, and later ordinary-message supersede cases.

### Expected

- Deferred direct turns resolve the visible deferral with a terminal message or abort cleanly.
- Later ordinary same-session messages do not cancel deferred finals.
- Explicit aborts still cancel pending deferred delivery.
- Group turns do not get direct-turn deferral diagnostics.

### Actual

- The focused regression suite passed all 118 assertions covering those cases.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Telegram/core Vitest suite, `git diff --check`, autoreview clean pass.
- Edge cases checked: stale deferral notices, final-before-deferral races, final-after-deferral ordering, normal-vs-abort supersede, fallback-send throw cleanup, group silence, and deferred NO_REPLY terminal completion.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram credentials, broad `pnpm check:changed` remote gate due local Testbox/AWS blockers.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A visible deferral could be sent for a run that later intentionally produces no visible assistant reply.
  - Mitigation: Once a deferral is visible, deferred silent completion now sends a terminal completion message instead of leaving the chat hanging.
- Risk: Deferred reply protection could accidentally ignore real aborts.
  - Mitigation: Reply-fence tests cover ordinary supersede protection separately from explicit abort behavior.
